programmes.show_description: Comedian Jon Richardson brings his sell-out live show to Blackpool's historic Grand Theatre, sharing his hilarious observations on daily life, from haircuts to crimes against dishwashing.
Sean Lock's stand-up is a blend of finely tuned hyperactive imagination, surreal imagery and insightful observations, from the price of cinema food to jewellery heists.
(2021) Feelgood coming-of-age musical with Max Harwood, Sarah Lancashire and Richard E Grant. A young lad dreams of escaping his humdrum existence and becoming a fabulous drag queen.
Angler's Lodge: Gordon Ramsay visits Angler's Lodge in Idaho, a stunning venue whose owners have been struck by family tragedy. Can he rescue the broken business?
Best friends Richard and Susan host at the Blackthorn Farm B&B on Anglesey, where some guests feel the vibes for their morning meditation, but others feel the squeeze in their bathroom.
Graham and Barbara pride themselves on offering basic facilities for great value at the White Swan Hotel in West Yorkshire. But will their guests be happy to pay a supplement for breakfast?
Malcolm and Fiona hope to show their guests an alternative way of life at The Old Mill House in Bridport, Dorset. But some aren't convinced and the meditation session gets a mixed response.
Rachel and Dan Lonergan host at The Brighton Inn, where the guests clown around as they learn some circus skills. But some of them are on the offensive...
It's the final day of the competition and the B&Bers meet for one last time to confront their rivals. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Doug and Carrie need some space but Arthur's in the way. So they hire a dog walker to amuse him for the day.  #WorldPoetryDay
Carrie needs some glasses but she can't quite make the leap. Doug helps out by buying her eye surgery on the cheap.  #WorldPoetryDay
Breakages are not OK. Doug's almost on his thousandth day, of zero packages destroyed. His depot colleagues are annoyed.  #WorldPoetryDay
Ray has a close encounter of the inappropriate kind: he goes to visit Peggy and she touches his behind.  #WorldPoetryDay
Debra is upset with Ray (we know: no great surprise). He's chosen the wrong partner to replace her if she dies.  #WorldPoetryDay
Debra plans a bridal shower, celebrating woman power. But Amy is left high and dry, when Debra gets a DUI.  #WorldPoetryDay
Cufflinks? In my bag; Oh, a secret admirer; But who and for whom? #WorldPoetryDay
Brother and dad now; What is a fella to do? Time for Shangri-la. #WorldPoetryDay
A dinner party; The gourmet society; But who ordered seal? #WorldPoetryDay
The Hateful 8-Year-Olds: Lisa attends a hellish sleepover, with seven penned in bambinos. It's less pillow fights and cute makeovers; more like a film by Quentin Tarantino. #WorldPoetryDay
Reverend Lovejoy's flock are depleting. He's got quite lazy at the preaching. Then a new priest in town, steals Lovejoy's crown, with his fresh take on religious teaching. #WorldPoetryDay
Springfield loves church, now Lovejoy is gone; well everyone other than Ned. He thinks the new priest's way of preaching is wrong - ecumenical troubles ahead! #WorldPoetryDay
One pair in Portsmouth are wowed by their room; another find the design delightful. But a duvet disaster could spell the host's doom, while the parking is utterly frightful. #WorldPoetryDay
The breakfast in Bournemouth is brimming with quality. But sod the high calibre; the guests just want quantity. #WorldPoetryDay
Paul and Tracey host in Dorset and aim to impress, with their fashionable hospitality. But substandard sleep leaves one guest depressed, and feedback presents abnormalities. #WorldPoetryDay
At a scenic glampsite in Cornwall, there are beautiful vistas for all. But dusty discoveries, leave some guests all snuffly - could this be the hosts' big downfall? #WorldPoetryDay
Finger-pointing, rows and venomous jibes; no this isn't Prime Minister's Questions. It's payment day on Four in a Bed - and there's plenty of feedback for digestion. #WorldPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: There once was a fellow called Phil. He and Kirstie were rather brill. At loving or listing. Using methods consisting. Of know-how and bargaining skill. #WorldPoetryDay
The search starts with a million-pound budget. Finding the right place; can Kirstie nudge it? And compromise is put to the test, when Rebecca and Sohail's kids fly the nest. #WorldPoetryDay
(1993) Classic romcom; love in the Big Apple. Long distance relationships can be such a battle.  Annie in Baltimore, already spoken for, and Sam is sleepless, up in Seattle. #WorldPoetryDay
Told without fear, told with courage, here's some in-depth current affairs coverage. #NationalPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: New: The Race for Ancient Egypt...
programmes.show_description: ...in Colour: Egypt takes control of its treasures, while experts race to find more. Extraordinary findings are made at Tanis. But then the world goes to war. (Ep2/2) #WorldPoetryDay

programmes.show_name: New: Roman Empire by Train with...
programmes.show_description: ...Alice Roberts: In Tarragona, Alice learns how the Romans gained control of the entire Med. In Cartagena, she marvels at subterranean riches of silver and lead. (Ep6/6) #WorldPoetryDay
In Hanoi, Bill explores Vietnam's complex relationship with life, death and war; a culture defined by spirituality, femininity and a unique tradition at its core. (Ep4) #WorldPoetryDay
(2015) The one where Cruise hangs off a plane just as it starts to fly. He does his stunts himself, y'know. I can't imagine why. #WorldPoetryDay
(2023) Horror at a resort in the sun. Foster's crime is a drunk hit-and-run. The sentence is strange, his new friends are deranged, and his punishment's barely begun. #WorldPoetryDay
Vienna Inn: Gordon checks in to the Vienna Inn, an Austrian-themed hotel and restaurant in Massachusetts, where business isn't what it used to be. Can he keep their doors open?
The handcuffed pairs go on a road trip, away from home and family, just the two of them and the iconic, idyllic great British outdoors. Naturally, tempers flare. (Ep5)
Shariqa and Imani have struggled to keep their Cambridgeshire renovation project on schedule. Can Stacey help them finally fly the nest? (Ep5/6)
In north London, Kirstie and Phil help first-time buyer Ify, as well as Mike and Rosemary, who've spent six years abroad and want to settle down.
Friender Bender: When Doug rams Kelly's car, he offers to pay for the damage, but Kelly presents him with an exorbitant bill. Meanwhile, Kirby terrorises Spence with peanuts.
No Retreat: Doug and Carrie go for a weekend away together, but Doug tells Carrie's colleagues a huge lie. Back in Queens, Arthur destroys Doug and Carrie's bedroom.
Ticker Treat: Doug festoons the house with Halloween decorations, even though Arthur has a phobia about it.
Baggage: Ray and Debra get involved in a two-week battle of wills over who's responsible for putting away a suitcase.
The Bachelor Party: Debra and Marie make Raymond feel guilty when they find out that the bachelor party he hosted for Robert was a huge disappointment.
Robert's Wedding (Part 1/2): Robert's suspicious of Peter's attempts to bury the hatchet, prompting him to ask Ray to keep an eye on him.
Roz, a Loan: Unemployed Roz is happy to hear that KACL might be rehiring everyone.
Merry Christmas, Mrs. Moskowitz: A friendly woman comes to Frasier's rescue when he's out Christmas shopping. He offers to return the favour, but it doesn't go as he expects...
The Good Samaritan: Frasier's arrested after unwittingly giving a ride to a sex worker. To make matters worse, he must explain the public scandal to his 11-year-old son.
Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen chats about his podcast. Babatunde Aléshé gives us the lowdown on the new series of Celebrity Bake Off. Plus: Ritu Arya, Greg Jenner and Foy Vance.
The Way of the Dog: When Santa's Little Helper bites Marge, the family explore his troubled past.
Bart of Darkness: Bart suspects that Ned Flanders has killed his wife when he spies on him through the rear window.
Lisa's Rival: Lisa is jealous of the new smart girl at school (guest star Winona Ryder) and conspires with Bart to wreck her entry to the Dioramarama competition.
Patricia and Mark have set their sights on moving to beautiful Lazio in Italy. Laura Hamilton shows them what £330k can buy, but will they find the perfect place?
A VW van gets a transformation with a twist, a brick bomb shelter is converted into a bijou bar, and George and Will forge ahead with their ultimate indoor-outdoor living project.
A steam engine enthusiast makes an ambitious journey across Devon in an 80-year-old steamroller. And two champion freedivers explore a spectacular underwater cave near Falmouth.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
(1995) Tom Hanks and Tim Allen star in the trailblazing, Oscar-winning family animation about toys that come to life and get into all kinds of scrapes when their kid's not looking.
Off for Stand Up To Cancer: Joe Wilkinson, Roisin Conaty, Rose Matafeo, Judi Love, Tom Davis and Jon Richardson recreate the weirdest places they've ever slept, in cake form!

programmes.show_name: New: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programmes.show_description: Epic adventure reality contest. In a vast, unforgiving forest, players are pitted against each other to win £100k. The Prey must hunt the money - while the Predators hunt the Prey... (Ep1)
This time the Goggleboxers watch Married at First Sight Australia, The Summit, LOL: Last One Laughing UK, Boarders, I Swear, Wheel of Fortune and Melania.
